title: AI Chief of Staff Skill-Orchestrated Agent Architecture
summary: Phase 1 and Phase 2 of the `AI Chief of Staff` architecture are implemented: the new `ai_chief_of_staff` composition root exists, the Chief of Staff skill registry/contract is live, and the builder can launch one assistant that wraps the existing follow-through, travel, and briefing systems as internal skills.
next_action: Start Phase 3 by introducing shared Chief of Staff acquisition/source-bundle modules and assistant-level arbitration so follow-through and travel can share fetch work and compete in one prioritization path.

verification.notes: `mix precommit` passed after shipping the first architecture slice. Phase 1 and Phase 2 are complete; shared acquisition, assistant-level arbitration, and broader product simplification remain for later work.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Shipped the first vertical slice: the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, skill registry/contract, adapter-backed followthrough/travel/briefing skills, builder wiring, and regression coverage.
pending_steps: Introduce shared Chief of Staff acquisition and source-bundle modules
Add assistant-level priority and interruption arbitration across skill outputs
Add assistant-origin metadata to persisted insights and briefs
Review whether the builder should expose skill toggles or keep the default skill pack fixed for the next phase
open_questions: Whether Slack follow-through should stay bundled inside the initial `followthrough` skill or become its own skill later
Whether assistant-level prioritization should take over Telegram staging directly in v1 or remain additive metadata first
Whether Phase 3 should deduplicate provider fetches through a shared bundle immediately or start with Gmail/Calendar sharing before Slack
resume_context.notes: The repo now has a working adapter-based `AI Chief of Staff` shell with builder support and passing verification. The next implementation phase should focus on the shared acquisition layer and assistant-level arbitration rather than rewriting the existing deterministic subsystems.

history.note: Initial draft created for a skill-oriented `AI Chief of Staff` architecture that consolidates travel, follow-through, and briefing under one user-facing assistant.

Implementation began for the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, the Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, adapter-based orchestration of the current travel and follow-through modules, and builder support for launching the unified assistant.

Completed the first architecture milestone: the `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, skill adapters for follow-through/travel/briefing, builder integration, and passing `mix precommit` verification.
